title: sigesbeckia pringlei subsp. tuerckheimii
intro: Sigesbeckia pringlei subsp. tuerckheimii, also known as Mexican or Yucatan marsh marigold, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of wounds. In herbal preparations, Sigesbeckia pringlei subsp. tuerckheimii is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
